name: Democratic Republic of the Congo
summary: The DRC is a country of immense strategic importance, possessing the world's largest reserves of cobalt and significant copper deposits critical to the global green energy transition. Under President Félix Tshisekedi, the country has maintained strong mining-led growth, though it faces a severe security crisis in the East involving the M23 insurgency and tensions with Rwanda. The government is currently focused on formalizing the mining sector, improving infrastructure through projects like the Lobito Corridor, and navigating complex regional mediation efforts.

politicalStability.note: High internal tension due to Eastern conflict and controversial 2023 election results.

economicDirection.note: Strong growth in mining, but high inflation and heavy reliance on commodity prices.

investmentAttractiveness.note: High interest in critical minerals; balanced by high risk and infrastructure deficits.

geopoliticalRisk.note: Active conflict with M23 rebels and severe diplomatic tension with Rwanda.

educationQuality.note: Free primary education policy has increased enrollment, but quality and funding remain low.

healthcareQuality.note: Struggles with recurrent disease outbreaks (Ebola, Mpox); urban facilities under strain.

technologyInvestment.note: Rising mobile penetration and digital ID initiatives; focus on rural connectivity.

relationships.summary: Dominant partner in the mining sector; recent renegotiation of the 'Sicomin' infrastructure-for-minerals deal.
Growing cooperation to secure critical mineral supply chains; backer of the Lobito Corridor.
Severe hostility; DRC accuses Rwanda of backing M23 rebels; high risk of regional conflict.
Key provider of peacekeeping troops through the SADC SAMIDRC mission.

perspectives.view: The copper boom is real, but the new cobalt quotas will force us to change how we operate.
We just want to go home. The politicians in Kinshasa and the world have forgotten us.
The prices of everything keep going up. The minerals are rich, but my pocket is empty.
We are re-asserting our sovereignty over our resources. The world needs our minerals, and they must pay a fair price.

historicalContext.summary: Since independence from Belgium in 1960, the DRC has faced decades of instability, including the long dictatorship of Mobutu Sese Seko and the devastating 'Great War of Africa' (1998-2003). It saw its first peaceful transition of power in 2019, though security challenges in the East remain a persistent legacy.

keyEvents.event: Mobutu Sese Seko overthrown; Laurent-Désiré Kabila takes power.
First peaceful transfer of power (Kabila to Tshisekedi).
Félix Tshisekedi re-elected in a landslide, disputed by opposition.
M23 rebels capture strategic hubs Goma and Bukavu.
DRC implements a cobalt export quota system to lift global prices.

strengths: Global leader in cobalt and copper production
Massive untapped hydroelectric and agricultural potential
Strategic importance for global green energy transition
risks: Active conflict and high insecurity in the East
Pervasive corruption and weak regulatory enforcement
Extreme reliance on global commodity price cycles
Vast infrastructure gaps in transport and energy
